verses.1: “I made a covenant with my eyes;
verses.2: For what is the portion from God above,
verses.3: Is it not calamity to the unrighteous,
verses.6: (let me be weighed in an even balance,
verses.7: if my step has turned out of the way,
verses.8: then let me sow, and let another eat.
verses.9: “If my heart has been enticed to a woman,
verses.10: then let my wife grind for another,
verses.11: For that would be a heinous crime.
verses.12: for it is a fire that consumes to destruction,
verses.13: “If I have despised the cause of my male servant
verses.14: what then will I do when God rises up?
verses.15: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him?
verses.16: “If I have withheld the poor from their desire,
verses.17: or have eaten my morsel alone,
verses.18: (no, from my youth he grew up with me as with a father,
verses.19: if I have seen any perish for want of clothing,
verses.20: if his heart hasn’t blessed me,
verses.21: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless,
verses.22: then let my shoulder fall from the shoulder blade,
verses.23: For calamity from God is a terror to me.
verses.24: “If I have made gold my hope,
verses.25: If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great,
verses.26: if I have seen the sun when it shined,
verses.27: and my heart has been secretly enticed,
verses.28: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges,
verses.29: “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me,
verses.30: (I have certainly not allowed my mouth to sin
verses.31: if the men of my tent have not said,
verses.32: (the foreigner has not camped in the street,
verses.33: if like Adam I have covered my transgressions,
verses.34: because I feared the great multitude,
verses.35: oh that I had one to hear me!
verses.36: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder,
verses.37: I would declare to him the number of my steps.
verses.38: If my land cries out against me,
verses.39: if I have eaten its fruits without money,
verses.40: let briers grow instead of wheat,
